skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation
skill.summary: Access 150+ serverless AI models through a unified CLI interface for image generation, video creation, LLM inference, web search, and automation tasks
skill.author: inference-sh-8

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 194 patterns in documentation files (markdown), not executable code. The 'backtick execution' and 'pipe to shell' findings are example commands in code blocks showing CLI usage. The skill restricts Bash access to 'infsh *' commands only via allowed-tools directive. API key access is documented and expected for authentication. No malicious intent detected after semantic review.

medium_findings.title: Shell Pipe Pattern in Installation Command
medium_findings.description: Documentation includes 'curl | sh' installation pattern which executes remote code directly. This is a common CLI distribution method but requires user trust in the source.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed in documentation as installation method. Legitimate for official CLI distribution but requires user awareness of risks.

low_findings.title: API Key Storage in Environment Variable
low_findings.description: Skill documents using INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for authentication. Standard practice but secrets should be handled carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Explicitly documented authentication method. Standard industry practice for API-based tools.

low_findings.title: External Network Dependencies
low_findings.description: Skill relies on external inference.sh infrastructure for all AI model execution. Service availability and data privacy depend on third-party.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Core functionality requires external API calls to inference.sh cloud infrastructure.

content.user_title: Execute 150+ Serverless AI Models via CLI
content.value_statement: Access enterprise AI capabilities without GPU infrastructure or model management. Run image generation, video creation, LLM inference, and web search through a unified command-line interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok Imagine, and Stable Diffusion models
Create videos with Veo 3.1, Seedance, Wan 2.5, and OmniHuman avatar models
Call LLMs including Claude Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, Gemini, Kimi, and GLM via OpenRouter
Perform web search and content extraction using Tavily and Exa APIs
Automate Twitter/X operations including tweets, likes, retweets, and DMs
limitations: Requires active internet connection for all operations
Depends on inference.sh cloud infrastructure availability
API usage subject to account quotas and credit balance
Cannot run custom models - limited to available inference.sh apps

use_cases.title: AI-Powered Content Creation
use_cases.description: Marketing teams generate promotional images, videos, and written content using multiple AI models without managing infrastructure or comparing different API providers.

use_cases.title: Rapid AI Prototyping
use_cases.description: Developers test different AI models for their applications by quickly switching between providers and model variants through unified commands.

use_cases.title: Automated Research Workflows
use_cases.description: Researchers automate literature searches, data extraction, and report generation by combining web search, LLM analysis, and document processing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a cat astronaut floating in space using the FLUX model

prompt_templates.title: Video Creation with Specific Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-second video of a drone flying over mountains at sunset using Veo 3.1, then save the result URL
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Video generation with parameter specification

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Research Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the latest AI news using Tavily, extract key points from the top 3 articles, and summarize them using Claude

prompt_templates.title: Automated Social Media Posting
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image for 'future of renewable energy', create an engaging caption using Gemini, and post to Twitter

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a sunset beach
Summarize quantum computing basics using Claude
output_examples.output: Quantum computing uses quantum bits (qubits) that can exist in superposition, enabling parallel computation. Key concepts include entanglement, interference, and quantum gates. Current applications focus on cryptography, optimization, and simulation.

best_practices: Always review generated content before using in production, especially for images and videos
Use --no-wait flag for long-running tasks and check status with task get command
Store API credentials securely using environment variables, never commit to version control
anti_patterns: Do not pass sensitive or private data to external AI models without reviewing privacy policies
Avoid running unverified apps from unknown developers in production workflows
Do not hardcode API keys in scripts or configuration files

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' then 'infsh login' to authenticate. Alternatively, download the binary manually from the releases page.

faq.question: What AI models are available?
faq.answer: 150+ models including FLUX for images, Veo for video, Claude/Gemini/Grok for text, Tavily/Exa for search, and specialized models for 3D, audio, and automation.

faq.question: How does pricing work?
faq.answer: Usage is based on credits consumed per model. Check your balance with 'infsh me' and view pricing on the inference.sh dashboard.

faq.question: Can I use this offline?
faq.answer: No, all model execution happens on inference.sh cloud infrastructure. An internet connection is required for all operations.

faq.question: How do I get my API key for CI/CD?
faq.answer: Generate an API key in your inference.sh dashboard settings, then set it as the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable in your CI environment.

faq.question: What formats are supported for outputs?
faq.answer: Images return as PNG/JPG URLs, videos as MP4/WebM, audio as WAV/MP3, and text as JSON. Use the --save flag to download files directly.
